name: Round Rock
card_summary: Round Rock roofing across the corridor from Old Settlers Park to Brushy Creek. Forest Creek, Behrens Ranch, Teravista, Paloma Lake, Sonoma. The Williamson County hail belt at its most exposed.
landmarks: Dell Diamond, Old Settlers Park, Brushy Creek Lake, Round Rock Premium Outlets, Kalahari Resort
neighborhoods: Forest Creek, Behrens Ranch, Teravista, Paloma Lake, Sonoma, Vista Oaks, Mayfield Ranch, Cat Hollow, Settlers Park, Greenhawe
climate_note: Round Rock catches the same Central Texas hail belt Austin does, but the open exposure between Brushy Creek and US 79 means wind events run measurably higher up here than they do in tree-canopied central Austin.
intro_paragraphs: Round Rock has been a roofing market since the corridor north of Brushy Creek started filling in through the late 1990s. Twenty-seven years of work for us in Central Texas means a lot of the Behrens Ranch, Forest Creek, and Teravista homes we are replacing now are second-roof installs. The original 1998 to 2005 builder-grade shingles are aging out at the same time. Round Rock's open exposure, more frequent hail events, and longer summer heat exposure on west-facing slopes all mean asphalt shingle lifecycles here run shorter than they do in central Austin. We see it in the free inspections we run every week.

We replace, repair, and inspect roofs across every Round Rock neighborhood. Forest Creek, Paloma Lake, Behrens Ranch, Teravista, Sonoma, Mayfield Ranch, Cat Hollow, Vista Oaks, and the older central Round Rock streets near Old Settlers Park. Same family-built crew. Same Benny on every estimate. Standing seam metal is increasingly the right call up here. The open exposure and hail frequency justify the system economically inside ten years, and most Round Rock insurance policies honor the Class 4 impact-rated discount of ten to twenty percent on the wind and hail portion of the premium.

Williamson County sits in the same hail belt as Travis County, and we have built every kind of insurance claim with State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Liberty Mutual, and the regional carriers that write business across 78664, 78665, and 78681. When the April 2024 storms crossed Round Rock with two and a half inch stones, we ran 47 free inspections across Forest Creek and Behrens Ranch in the eight days that followed. Most of those homes had hail damage. About eighty percent of the replacements closed insurance-covered minus deductible.
what_we_do_locally: Roof replacement on second-roof builder homes from the 1998 to 2008 Round Rock build-out across Forest Creek, Behrens Ranch, Teravista, Sonoma, and Paloma Lake
Hail damage inspections after every confirmed Williamson County event, averaging three to five per year
Standing seam metal upgrades suited to Round Rock's open exposure, with Class 4 insurance discount documentation delivered on close-out
Storm damage and 24-hour emergency tarp service across the corridor
Insurance claim filing support and adjuster meetings on the roof
City of Round Rock permits pulled and inspections coordinated

faqs.q: Do you serve all of Round Rock, TX?
faqs.a: Yes. We work every Round Rock zip code: 78664, 78665, and 78681. Forest Creek, Behrens Ranch, Teravista, Paloma Lake, Sonoma, Mayfield Ranch, Cat Hollow, Vista Oaks, and the older central Round Rock streets near Old Settlers Park. Same family-built crew, same pricing as our Austin work, same Benny walking the estimate.

faqs.q: Is Round Rock hail damage covered by Texas homeowners insurance?
faqs.a: Yes. Most Texas homeowners policies cover Round Rock hail damage subject to your deductible. Williamson County sits in the same hail belt as Travis County, and every major Texas carrier already rates that risk into the premium. We meet your State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, or other adjuster on the roof and file supplements when code upgrades, decking, drip edge, or ice and water shield items are missed at the initial inspection. About eighty percent of our Round Rock hail damage replacements close insurance-covered after the homeowner's deductible.

faqs.q: How long does a Round Rock roof replacement take?
faqs.a: Most Round Rock asphalt shingle replacements run one to two days for a typical 2,000 to 2,500 square foot home. Standing seam metal runs two to four days. We schedule around your work week, pull City of Round Rock permits ahead of the install, and deliver materials the day before the crew arrives.

faqs.q: Why do Round Rock roofs fail faster than Austin roofs?
faqs.a: Three reasons specific to Williamson County geography. The open exposure north of Brushy Creek catches wind and hail with significantly less tree canopy than central Austin. Most Round Rock subdivisions built between 1998 and 2008 used builder-grade three-tab shingles with twenty-year warranties that are now aging out at sixteen to twenty years. And the corridor sees slightly higher summer roof surface temperatures on west-facing slopes. The combination ages asphalt shingle two to four years faster than the same product in central Austin.

faqs.q: Do you pull Round Rock permits and handle inspections?
faqs.a: Yes. We pull all required permits with the City of Round Rock before the install starts. Most residential re-roofs require a permit. We coordinate the City inspection on close-out. The permit cost is built into your quote.

faqs.q: What is the best roofing system for a Round Rock home?
faqs.a: For most Round Rock homes, standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume offers the best economic case. The Class 4 impact rating qualifies for a ten to twenty percent insurance discount, the forty to fifty year service life eliminates the next replacement cycle, and the open exposure that ages asphalt here does not affect metal panels. Architectural asphalt remains the most common choice at the $8,000 to $18,000 range. Decra stone-coated steel and DaVinci synthetic slate are common premium upgrades on the Behrens Ranch and Teravista custom builds.

name: Cedar Park
card_summary: Cedar Park roofing across Twin Creeks, Ranch at Brushy Creek, Buttercup Creek, Anderson Mill, and the 183A corridor. IKO Master, Decra Certified, DaVinci Masterpiece. Few crews in the 78613 carry all three.
landmarks: H-E-B Center, Brushy Creek Regional Trail, Cedar Park Town Center, Twin Creeks Country Club, 1890 Ranch
neighborhoods: Buttercup Creek, Anderson Mill, Twin Creeks, Ranch at Brushy Creek, Heritage Park, Forest Oaks, Cypress Canyon, Reserve at Brushy Creek, Brushy Creek Meadows
climate_note: Cedar Park sits on the edge of Hill Country exposure. West-facing roofs catch the full afternoon Texas sun and UV degradation runs measurably faster here. The Buttercup Creek elevation also means wind events register harder than they do in basin neighborhoods east of MoPac.
intro_paragraphs: Cedar Park has one of the highest concentrations of premium tile, Decra, and DaVinci installs in the Austin metro. The custom builds across Twin Creeks, Ranch at Brushy Creek, Reserve at Brushy Creek, and the newer Heritage Park sections specify the upgrade systems by design. The west-facing exposure that defines so much of Cedar Park's lot geometry also means the longer-life systems pay back faster up here than they do in central Austin. UV degradation hits architectural shingle hardest on west elevations.

We install every Cedar Park system. Architectural asphalt for the workhorse roof at $8,000 to $18,000. Snap-lock standing seam metal for the modern build at $14,000 to $28,000. Decra stone-coated steel for the long-haul homeowner at $18,000 to $32,000. DaVinci synthetic slate for the historic-aesthetic builds in the older Anderson Mill streets at $24,000 to $48,000. We hold the master tier with IKO, Decra, and DaVinci. Few crews in the 78613 zip carry all three credentials. The credential matters because the full manufacturer warranty requires certified installation.

We work directly with the major insurance carriers across Williamson County. State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Liberty Mutual. Hail damage claims are common here. The April 2024 storms produced 2.5 to 4 inch stones across north Cedar Park and the 183A corridor. About eighty percent of our Cedar Park hail damage replacements close insurance-covered minus the homeowner's deductible.
what_we_do_locally: Premium tile, Decra, and DaVinci installs across the Twin Creeks, Ranch at Brushy Creek, and Reserve at Brushy Creek custom builds
Standing seam metal upgrades on the west-facing Hill Country exposure that ages shingle fastest
Hail and wind damage inspections after every storm crossing the 183A corridor
Custom-build roofing for the higher-end Cedar Park subdivisions where HOA covenants require premium materials
Architectural shingle replacement on the 2000 to 2010 Buttercup Creek and Anderson Mill build-out
City of Cedar Park and Williamson County permits pulled and inspections coordinated

faqs.q: Do you cover every Cedar Park neighborhood?
faqs.a: Yes. Buttercup Creek, Anderson Mill, Twin Creeks, Ranch at Brushy Creek, Reserve at Brushy Creek, Heritage Park, Forest Oaks, Cypress Canyon, Brushy Creek Meadows, and the older central Cedar Park streets. Every zip code in 78613 and 78630.

faqs.q: Is Decra stone-coated steel worth the premium in Cedar Park?
faqs.a: Often yes. Decra runs about sixty percent more than architectural asphalt shingle on a comparable Cedar Park install. It carries a fifty-year manufacturer warranty, Class 4 impact rating, and qualifies for a ten to twenty percent Texas homeowners insurance discount. The recovery window on a typical Cedar Park home is eleven to fourteen years between the insurance discount and the avoided replacement, then you have thirty-five plus years of zero re-roof spend. For Cedar Park homeowners staying in the home long-term, the math usually works. We will run it with you honestly at the quote.

faqs.q: What is the right roofing system for Hill Country Cedar Park?
faqs.a: Concrete and clay tile installs are common across Twin Creeks and Ranch at Brushy Creek where structural framing supports the load. Standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume is the best economic case for most homes on west-facing lots because the Class 4 rating earns the insurance discount and the panel survives the Hill Country UV exposure. DaVinci synthetic slate works on the smaller historic-aesthetic builds in Anderson Mill where the framing was not engineered for real tile weight.

faqs.q: Do you pull permits in Cedar Park?
faqs.a: Yes. We pull City of Cedar Park or Williamson County permits as required by the subdivision and project scope. Most residential re-roofs need them. The permit cost is built into your quote. We coordinate the inspection on close-out.

faqs.q: How does Cedar Park hail damage compare to Round Rock?
faqs.a: Similar frequency, slightly different geography. Cedar Park's elevation along the 183A corridor and west-facing exposure means hail and wind events that come off the Hill Country tend to hit harder here. We see slightly more wind damage in Cedar Park, slightly more pure hail damage in Round Rock. Both sit firmly in the Central Texas hail belt and both qualify for full hail damage coverage under standard Texas homeowners policies.

faqs.q: Why do Cedar Park roofs need replacement sooner than central Austin?
faqs.a: Hill Country exposure. West-facing slopes in Cedar Park see significantly more direct UV than tree-canopied central Austin. Architectural shingle that should last twenty-five years here often needs replacement at seventeen to twenty. Tile and metal systems are not affected by UV the same way and are why we recommend the upgrade systems on most Cedar Park west-facing builds.

name: Tarrytown
card_summary: Tarrytown roofing across 1920s Pemberton Heights bungalows, Old Enfield blocks, and the Bryker Woods cottages. Skip-sheathing, hand-cut rafters, period-correct DaVinci synthetic slate restoration. This is where the craft lives.
landmarks: Lake Austin, Pemberton Heights Historic District, Reed Park, Camp Mabry, Mayfield Park, MoPac Boulevard
neighborhoods: Pemberton Heights, Old Enfield, Bryker Woods, Westover Hills, Tarry Hill, West Austin, Niles Road
climate_note: Tarrytown's mature oak and pecan canopy is the densest in central Austin. The canopy protects from direct UV and most hail events but drops constant debris and produces uneven shingle aging patterns where one shaded slope can outlive its sun-exposed neighbor by five to seven years.
intro_paragraphs: Tarrytown is where the craft lives. The 1920s and 30s Pemberton Heights bungalows, the older Enfield Road blocks, the Bryker Woods cottages. Skip-sheathing under the decking, hand-cut rafters, original cedar shake that has been over-roofed twice across the past century. The homes here punish the wrong crew and reward the right one. We have been roofing Tarrytown and the 78703 zip for over two decades. We know what every era of build is hiding under the layers before we lift the first shingle.

We do period-correct restoration on the historic builds. DaVinci synthetic slate for the heritage aesthetic without the historic weight load most Tarrytown framing was never engineered to carry. Custom copper-leaded flashings on the older chimneys, hand-folded on your driveway. Real clay tile re-laid by hand on the Spanish Colonial revivals. We also install modern asphalt shingle and snap-lock standing seam metal on the newer Tarrytown infill builds, same family-built crew, same Benny on the estimate.

Two hundred plus historic-era roofs in Tarrytown, Pemberton Heights, Bryker Woods, and Old Enfield over the past two decades. We hold the DaVinci Masterpiece Contractor credential, which matters because the full DaVinci warranty requires certified installation to register. We also handle the City of Austin historic-overlay submission process for the blocks that fall under it. Some of the Pemberton Heights and Old Enfield streets require material approval for any visible roof change. We submit and handle the approval as part of the project.
what_we_do_locally: Period-correct restoration on the 1920s and 30s Pemberton Heights, Old Enfield, and Bryker Woods bungalows
DaVinci synthetic slate installs for the heritage aesthetic without the historic weight load
Skip-sheathing decking replacement on the historic builds where the original spacing does not meet current Austin code
Custom copper and lead flashings on the historic chimneys, hand-folded on your driveway
Real clay tile re-lay on Spanish Colonial revivals
City of Austin historic-overlay submission and approval handling for blocks that require it

faqs.q: Do you have experience on Tarrytown historic homes?
faqs.a: Yes. Over two hundred historic-era roofs in Tarrytown, Pemberton Heights, Bryker Woods, and Old Enfield across the past two decades. We know skip-sheathing, period-correct material selection, custom copper flashings, hand-cut rafter detail, and the City of Austin historic-overlay rules that some of these blocks fall under.

faqs.q: Can you match the original Tarrytown roof aesthetic?
faqs.a: Yes. DaVinci synthetic slate matches the look of natural slate from the street without the historic weight load most Tarrytown framing was never engineered to carry. Real clay tile we re-lay by hand for the Spanish Colonial revivals. Cedar shake we restore where the underlying framing allows or replace with synthetic shake that holds the period look and passes current Austin fire code.

faqs.q: Do Tarrytown roofs need historic overlay approval?
faqs.a: Some do. The blocks immediately around Pemberton Heights and parts of Old Enfield sit under City of Austin historic-overlay rules that require material approval for any visible roof change. We handle the approval submission as part of the project at no extra cost to the homeowner. Most overlay approvals come back inside three weeks.

faqs.q: What is special about Tarrytown's tree canopy and roofing?
faqs.a: The dense oak and pecan canopy across most of Tarrytown protects roofs from direct UV and softens most hail events. That extends shingle life by three to seven years compared to unshaded suburban builds. The canopy also drops debris constantly, creates uneven aging patterns where one shaded slope ages thirty percent slower than its neighbor, and produces branch-impact damage that most insurance policies cover. We inspect for branch-impact damage specifically on Tarrytown homes after any wind event.

faqs.q: How long do Tarrytown roofs last?
faqs.a: Depending on system and exposure. Architectural asphalt under heavy canopy can run eighteen to twenty-five years here because the canopy extends shingle life by reducing UV exposure. DaVinci synthetic slate carries a fifty-year manufacturer warranty. Real clay tile lasts fifty plus years if the underlayment is refreshed at the thirty-year mark. Standing seam metal runs forty to fifty years.

faqs.q: What does a DaVinci synthetic slate roof cost in Tarrytown?
faqs.a: DaVinci synthetic slate on a typical 2,000 to 2,500 square foot Tarrytown bungalow runs $30,000 to $55,000 installed depending on roof complexity, decking condition, and which DaVinci profile you choose. The fifty-year warranty, Class 4 impact rating, and historic-aesthetic match make it the right call for most Pemberton Heights and Old Enfield restorations. We are DaVinci Masterpiece installers, which is required for the full warranty to register on the home.

name: Westlake Hills
card_summary: Westlake Hills custom builds across Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, Rob Roy, and the Tarrytown West blocks. Decra, DaVinci, real concrete tile, snap-lock standing seam in 22-gauge. The work that justifies the master-tier certifications.
landmarks: Lake Austin, Eanes ISD, Bee Caves Road, Mount Bonnell, Davenport Ranch, 360 Bridge
neighborhoods: Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, Rob Roy, Westlake Estates, Tarrytown West, Rollingwood-adjacent, Westlake Heights
climate_note: Westlake Hills sits firmly in Hill Country exposure. West-facing roofs catch the full Texas sun for six to eight hours daily. The elevated lot geometry across Davenport Ranch and Lost Creek means wind events register harder than they do in basin neighborhoods, and UV degradation runs faster on every west elevation.
intro_paragraphs: Westlake Hills is custom builds, custom geometry, custom materials. Most of what we do in the 78746 is premium systems on first-roof installs at the twenty-five-year mark, or full replacements on twenty-five-year-old original systems that have aged through one Central Texas climate cycle. Decra stone-coated steel, real concrete tile, snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume, DaVinci synthetic slate on the historic-aesthetic builds. The Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, and Rob Roy roofs are not architectural asphalt jobs and never should be.

We hold the master tier on IKO, Decra, and DaVinci. Few Austin crews carry all three. Snap-lock standing seam in 22-gauge G90 galvalume with concealed-fastener systems where no screw penetrates a panel face. Real concrete tile from Eagle, Westile, and Boral installed to manufacturer specification on framing inspected for the load. DaVinci synthetic slate on the smaller historic-aesthetic builds. This is the work that justifies the master-tier credentials. Westlake Hills homes built right outlive the homeowner.

Most Westlake Hills HOAs have material covenants. Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, and parts of Rob Roy require tile, metal, or comparable premium systems. We handle HOA submission and approval as part of the project. We also work directly with every major Texas insurance carrier on hail and wind damage claims across the 78746. State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Chubb, Pure, and the regional carriers that write high-value coverage in West Austin all sit inside our normal workflow.
what_we_do_locally: Decra stone-coated steel on the Davenport Ranch and Lost Creek custom builds
Real concrete tile from Eagle, Westile, and Boral on Hill Country builds with the structural framing for the load
Snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume with hand-folded concealed-fastener transitions
DaVinci synthetic slate on the historic-aesthetic builds
Custom flashings, valleys, and complex multi-elevation geometry hand-built on site
HOA submission and approval handling for Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, Rob Roy, and other covenanted Westlake subdivisions

faqs.q: Why is Westlake Hills roofing different from central Austin?
faqs.a: Three reasons. Geometry, exposure, and material specification. Most Westlake Hills builds have complex multi-elevation rooflines with custom valleys, dormers, integrated chimneys, and turret elements that simpler builds do not. The west-facing Hill Country exposure means UV degradation runs measurably faster than central Austin. And most homes are specified for premium systems by design or by HOA covenant, which requires master-tier certified installers for the full manufacturer warranty to register.

faqs.q: Do Westlake Hills roofs have to be tile?
faqs.a: Not always. Some subdivisions including Davenport Ranch and Lost Creek have HOA covenants requiring tile, metal, or comparable premium systems. Others allow architectural asphalt but the property value premium of a tile, Decra, or standing seam metal roof in the 78746 is significant. We give you the honest answer for your specific build and your specific HOA at the free inspection.

faqs.q: What does a Westlake Hills roof replacement cost?
faqs.a: Premium systems on a Westlake Hills custom build run $35,000 to $85,000 for real concrete tile from Eagle, Westile, or Boral. $30,000 to $70,000 for Decra stone-coated steel. $24,000 to $55,000 for snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge. $40,000 to $100,000+ for natural slate or DaVinci synthetic slate. The range is wide because Westlake Hills roofs have wide square footage and wide geometric complexity ranges. We quote firm in writing after the on-site inspection.

faqs.q: Does the Class 4 insurance discount apply in Westlake Hills?
faqs.a: Yes. Class 4 impact-rated systems including Decra stone-coated steel, snap-lock standing seam metal, DaVinci synthetic slate, and most concrete tiles qualify for a ten to twenty percent discount on the wind and hail portion of the homeowners premium on every major Texas carrier including State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Chubb, and Pure. We document the rating in writing and hand-deliver the letter the day we finish.

faqs.q: How long does a Westlake Hills tile or metal roof last?
faqs.a: Real concrete and clay tile last fifty plus years if the underlayment is refreshed at the thirty-year mark. Decra stone-coated steel carries a fifty-year manufacturer warranty. Snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge runs forty to fifty years. DaVinci synthetic slate carries a fifty-year warranty. Most Westlake Hills premium roofs outlive the homeowner if the install crew put them in correctly.

faqs.q: Do you handle HOA approval submissions in Westlake Hills?
faqs.a: Yes. Davenport Ranch, Lost Creek, Rob Roy, and most Westlake Hills subdivisions require HOA material approval before any roof replacement. We submit the application, provide the architectural review samples, and handle the approval timeline as part of the project. Most HOA approvals come back inside three to five weeks.

name: Bee Cave
card_summary: Bee Cave Hill Country roofing across Falconhead, Sweetwater, Spanish Oaks, Bella Colinas, and the 71/620 corridor. Premium systems on the 2005 to 2018 builds reaching first replacement now.
landmarks: Hill Country Galleria, Highway 71, Highway 620, Lake Travis, Falconhead, Backyard Amphitheater
neighborhoods: Falconhead, Falconhead West, Lake Pointe, Bella Colinas, Sweetwater, Spanish Oaks, Homestead, Vista Pointe
climate_note: Bee Cave sits in pure Hill Country exposure. West-facing roofs catch the full Texas sun without the central Austin tree canopy that softens UV. The 71/620 corridor elevation means wind events come down the Hill Country edge harder than they do at lower elevations.
intro_paragraphs: Bee Cave is the newer Hill Country build-out. Falconhead, Sweetwater, Spanish Oaks, Bella Colinas, Lake Pointe, and Homestead. Most homes up here were built between 2005 and 2018, which means we are now seeing the first major re-roof wave on the early builds. Builder-grade architectural shingle that came with a twenty to twenty-five year warranty is aging out under sharper UV exposure and harder wind events than the manufacturer data sheets accounted for. We replace these roofs every week.

Most Bee Cave first-replacement roofs deserve a system upgrade. Snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume. Decra stone-coated steel. Real concrete tile on the Spanish Oaks and Bella Colinas builds where the structural framing supports the load. We hold the master tier on IKO, Decra, and DaVinci. Few Hill Country crews carry all three credentials. The credential matters because the full manufacturer warranty requires certified installation to register on the home.

We work directly with every Texas carrier across the 78738. State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Liberty Mutual, Chubb, and the regional carriers that write business along the Lake Travis corridor. Hail and wind damage are common here. The April 2024 storms produced 2.5 to 4 inch stones across the 71/620 corridor. We inspected 32 Bee Cave properties in the eight days that followed and most had documentable damage. About eighty percent of those replacements closed insurance-covered minus the homeowner's deductible.
what_we_do_locally: First-replacement upgrades on the 2005 to 2018 Bee Cave builds across Falconhead, Sweetwater, Spanish Oaks, and Bella Colinas
Snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume suited to Hill Country UV exposure
Decra stone-coated steel and real concrete tile on the premium builds where HOA covenants require them
Hail and wind damage inspections after every confirmed Travis County event crossing the 71/620 corridor
Custom geometry work on the multi-elevation Hill Country rooflines
City of Bee Cave and Travis County permits pulled and inspections coordinated

faqs.q: Do you cover every Bee Cave neighborhood?
faqs.a: Yes. Falconhead, Falconhead West, Lake Pointe, Bella Colinas, Sweetwater, Spanish Oaks, Homestead, Vista Pointe, and the smaller subdivisions along the 71/620 corridor. Every 78738 build. Same family-built crew, same Benny walking the estimate.

faqs.q: Why upgrade the roof system at first replacement in Bee Cave?
faqs.a: Bee Cave's Hill Country exposure hits builder-grade architectural shingle harder than the manufacturer data sheets account for. Most first replacements happen at sixteen to twenty years here, not the twenty-five years the warranty implied. Upgrading to snap-lock standing seam metal or Decra stone-coated steel at first replacement extends the next service life to forty to fifty years, qualifies you for the Class 4 insurance discount of ten to twenty percent on the wind and hail portion of the premium, and most Bee Cave homeowners stay in the home long enough to recover the cost differential inside ten to fourteen years.

faqs.q: Is hail damage a real concern in Bee Cave?
faqs.a: Yes. The 71/620 corridor catches hail events that come down the Hill Country edge with regularity. We inspect for free across the 78738 within 48 hours of any confirmed Travis County hail event, document any damage to insurance standard, help file claims, and meet your adjuster on the roof. About eighty percent of our Bee Cave hail damage claims close insurance-covered after the homeowner's deductible.

faqs.q: Do you pull Bee Cave permits?
faqs.a: Yes. Permits as required by the City of Bee Cave or Travis County depending on subdivision and project scope. We coordinate the inspection on close-out. The permit cost is included in your quote.

faqs.q: How does Bee Cave roofing compare to Westlake Hills?
faqs.a: Geographically similar, both in Hill Country exposure with west-facing UV pressure and elevated wind risk. Bee Cave has a higher concentration of 2005 to 2018 builds reaching first replacement right now, while Westlake Hills has more 1990s and earlier custom builds at second or third replacement. The work product is comparable. The crew is the same. Pricing runs slightly lower in Bee Cave because of less complex geometry on the production builds versus the full-custom Westlake homes.

faqs.q: What is the best roofing system for a Bee Cave home?
faqs.a: For most Bee Cave homes built between 2005 and 2018, snap-lock standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume offers the best long-term economic case. The Class 4 impact rating qualifies for a ten to twenty percent insurance discount. The forty to fifty year service life eliminates the next replacement cycle. And the panel survives Hill Country UV exposure that ages architectural shingle measurably faster. Decra stone-coated steel and real concrete tile are common upgrades on the Spanish Oaks and Bella Colinas custom builds where HOA covenants require them.
